section_miscategorizations.text: He presents with elevated blood pressure and mild hyperglycemia.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This sentence is framed as a presentation of objective findings (vital sign and lab/glucose status) rather than an interpretive diagnostic impression. It would be clearer in the Objective section, but its placement in Assessment is not seriously misleading.

This appears to describe an existing antihypertensive regimen rather than a new or changed order. If it reflects current therapy being documented, it belongs in Subjective; if it is an explicit continuation order, then Plan is acceptable. The intent is ambiguous, so this is a minor organizational issue.
